stories.title: Reader choices now constrain the next review.
stories.brief: Baxtori exports one GitHub account's state, reviews only selected configured repositories, and preserves repository-specific re-review guidance.
stories.verdict: Worth studying: the reading interface now closes the loop with the compiler
stories.whatChanged: Account-backed reader state and versioned re-review requests were added in Convex, then connected to export, collection, and exact-ID completion scripts. The collector also switched to fetched origin branches and intersects account selections with configured caches.
stories.whyItMatters: A weekly review can now follow the reader's repository choices and explicit lens without broadening scope or confusing one story's guidance with another repository.
stories.verify: Select one configured repository, queue a re-review with a distinctive lens, run feedback:export and backstory:collect, then confirm candidates.json contains only that repository and the unchanged request fields.
stories.tradeoff: The automation boundary depends on a shared Convex secret, and compiler lookup currently scans reader states by GitHub login before selecting the newest match; that is acceptable at personal scale but not a scalable identity index.
stories.evidence: 1 origin-backed feature commit · account state, review queue, export, scoped collection, and completion

codeEvidence.title: Keep each re-review repository-specific
codeEvidence.why: The queued record stores the repository, lens instruction, guidance, edition, and story together, while a newer request only supersedes the same story in the same edition.

codeEvidence.title: Export one account's compiler input
codeEvidence.why: The scratch input is queried by FEEDBACK_GITHUB_LOGIN and carries the selected account state plus queued requests in creation order.

codeEvidence.title: Intersect reader intent with configured caches
codeEvidence.why: Selection narrows the configured source manifest, and unconfigured choices are reported separately instead of being fabricated or fetched from an unregistered path.

stories.title: Captured originals prefer a portable home.
stories.brief: Ourchival uploads fetched images to private Google Drive, records provider metadata, and falls back to Convex Storage or the original link.
stories.verdict: Worth studying: storage failure degrades without discarding reference context
stories.whatChanged: The capture path gained a Drive OAuth helper, multipart uploads tagged with their source URL, provider-aware asset records, and a Convex Storage fallback when Drive is unavailable.
stories.whyItMatters: Original files can live in a user-controlled Drive folder while the searchable Convex catalog retains source and storage provenance. A failed upload does not erase the reference itself.
stories.verify: Capture the same small remote image once with Drive credentials and once without them; confirm the two asset records report google_drive and convex while both references retain their source URL.
stories.tradeoff: The committed capture and Drive proxy routes allow broad cross-origin access without an authentication gate, so the storage design is portable but the deployed privacy boundary is still unfinished.
stories.evidence: 2 origin-backed commits · Drive multipart upload plus provider-aware fallback in the capture action

codeEvidence.title: Upload source-tagged originals to Drive
codeEvidence.why: The helper requires explicit OAuth configuration, writes the source URL into Drive app properties, and returns a visible failure status rather than pretending the upload succeeded.

codeEvidence.title: Fall back without losing storage provenance
codeEvidence.why: The capture action prefers a successful Drive upload, otherwise stores the same blob in Convex, and marks unfetchable assets as linked instead of dropping the reference.

stories.title: Combat pressure became measurable without changing combat.
stories.brief: A log-derived report turns deterministic fights into rounds, health pressure, technique signals, and grades across repeatable simulations.
stories.verdict: Worth studying: observational telemetry preserves the engine boundary
stories.whatChanged: A standalone parser reconstructs fight results from engine logs, grades their pressure, aggregates named technique signals, and runs the report over seeded road simulations with synthetic fight tests.
stories.whyItMatters: Balance discussion can distinguish dominant, narrow, battered, and routed fights instead of relying only on overall win rate, without coupling reporting rules into the deterministic engine.
stories.verify: Run npm run test:pressure and OML_PRESSURE_RUNS=50 npm run report:pressure twice; the synthetic cases should pass and each seeded report should repeat exactly.
stories.tradeoff: The report parses human-readable combat prose, so copy changes can silently alter telemetry; the committed handoff correctly treats a first-class FightResult engine contract as the likely next boundary.
stories.evidence: 1 origin-backed commit · parser, deterministic simulation, synthetic combat cases, and scheduled report workflow

codeEvidence.title: Reconstruct and grade fight boundaries
codeEvidence.why: The parser finalizes fights at explicit victory and defeat log entries, carries gauntlet health forward, and derives pressure grades from remaining health, damage, and rounds.

codeEvidence.title: Measure pressure over deterministic roads
codeEvidence.why: The harness reuses the headless engine, fixed seed sequence, and explicit bot choices before aggregating every reconstructed fight by road.

codeEvidence.title: Pin the prose-to-telemetry contract
codeEvidence.why: Synthetic logs assert grades, technique markers, win counts, and health carry between bouts, making the parser's fragile textual assumptions executable.
